content: Just as the language learning sense of 'reflection' as a deliberate, metacognitive practice to reinforce vocabulary and comprehension improves human learning, the orthographic variant 'reflexion' (the older, less commonly used spelling of reflection) can be leveraged as a distinct anchor for LLM self-reflection mechanisms to separate naive initial outputs from deliberate revised reasoning. This orthographic distinction creates a natural discrete memory marker that helps the LLM dynamically categorize its own thinking steps without complex additional memory architecture.
